name: Self-Help Credit Union
description_short: Self-Help Credit Union is a federally insured credit union offering checking, savings, loans, and banking services with branches across multiple states including a Jacksonville, FL location.
description_long: Self-Help Credit Union operates as a multi-state credit union with a presence in North Carolina, Virginia, South Carolina, Florida, and Georgia, plus additional locations in California, Illinois, Washington, and Wisconsin. The organization has been serving members with traditional and specialized financial products for consumer and small business needs. At their Jacksonville (Kendall) branch location, members can access comprehensive banking services including account opening, checking and savings accounts, personal loans, home loans, and small business financing. The credit union distinguishes itself through offering free electric vehicle (EV) charging at their Jacksonville branch, demonstrating commitment to emerging consumer needs and sustainability. Unlike many traditional banks, Self-Help operates as a member-owned institution with a focus on financial accessibility. The organization maintains multiple service channels including branch locations with drive-thru service, a toll-free phone line, online account access with mobile app functionality, and ATM networks. For consumers seeking traditional banking services with credit union member benefits, Self-Help provides a legitimate alternative to commercial banks with a multi-state footprint. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Member-owned credit union structure with federally insured deposits
Free EV charging available at Jacksonville Kendall branch
Multiple service channels: branch, drive-thru, phone (800-966-7353), online, and mobile app
Comprehensive loan products including personal, home, and small business loans
Multi-state presence across 8+ states providing geographic flexibility
Extended hours on select days (some branches open 10 AM-5 PM Wednesday)
Account opening and checking/savings options available at branch locations
cons: Limited branch locations compared to national banks; gaps in geographic coverage
Website navigation issues with broken/404 pages in service sections
No specific information about loan rates, terms, or APR details available
Limited transparency on membership eligibility requirements and fee structures
best_for: Credit union members seeking multi-state banking access and member benefits
EV owners in Jacksonville wanting free charging with banking services
Small business owners needing personal and business lending relationships
Consumers prioritizing member-owned financial institutions over corporate banks
diagnosis: Self-Help Credit Union is best for consumers who value credit union membership benefits and need access to banking services across multiple states. The primary caveat is that loan terms, rates, and eligibility requirements are not transparently detailed on their website, requiring direct contact to evaluate product competitiveness.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
